title: orobanche minor subsp. ozanonis
intro: Orobanche minor subsp. ozanonis is a subspecies of Orobanche minor, also known as the small broomrape, a type of parasitic plant that derives its nutrients by attaching to the roots of its host plant. Traditionally, Orobanche minor subsp. ozanonis has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Orobanche minor subsp. ozanonis are said to include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Orobanche minor subsp. ozanonis is prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
